tracks.meaning: the act of moving something or someone from one place to another
the movement of money from one account, person, or institution to another
the process of moving data or files from one storage location or device to another
a shift in a player's position or assignment during a game or season
the legal conveyance of property, rights, or ownership from one party to another
the application of knowledge or skills learned in one context to a different context
the relocation of a patient from one facility, unit, or care setting to another
the movement of troops, equipment, or military personnel from one location to another
the movement of a student from one school, course, or educational program to another
the reassignment of an employee to a different position, department, or location
the transmission or relaying of information, messages, or design from one person or medium to another
the process of moving an image or design from one surface to another using pressure or heat
the exchange or sending of information between devices, systems, or networks
a movement or shift from one state, position, or condition to another
to move oneself or change one's circumstances in a decisive or purposeful way
